entry.title: MAD-HTLC
entry.summary:
In the discussion surrounding Bitcoin mining strategies, it's emphasized that rational and non-myopic miners would inherently support a particular type of attack without needing to cooperate explicitly. This scenario hinges on the assumption that all miners understand each other's rationality and long-term perspective. For those seeking security against such potential coordinated actions, the use of mad-htlc is suggested as a more secure alternative to the traditional htlc method. This recommendation comes with the caveat that any deviation from this assumption likely involves some degree of altruism or less-than-optimal strategy implementation among the miners.
Further elaboration clarifies the distinction between myopic and non-myopic behavior in miners. The term 'non-myopic', as used in the discourse, implies a strategic depth where miners looking beyond immediate gains will not opt for inferior strategies compared to their myopic counterparts, who focus on short-term outcomes. It is generally agreed upon that if a significant number of miners are either not rational or are myopic, and the incentive (bribe) is minimal, both myopic and non-myopic miners would find it strategically sound to reject the bribe. This consensus highlights an understanding of strategic behaviors based on the perceived time horizon and rationality of the miners involved.
